nl: List all customers
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM customers

nl: Show total number of orders
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders

nl: What is the total revenue of all orders?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders

nl: List all completed orders
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

nl: Count the number of products in Electronics category
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products WHERE category = 'Electronics'

nl: What is the average price of products?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(list_price) FROM products

nl: Show names and emails of all customers in the UK
expected_sql: SELECT name, email FROM customers WHERE country = 'UK'

nl: List all order items with quantity greater than 2
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM order_items WHERE quantity > 2

nl: Show products that have a list price over $100
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE list_price > 100

nl: Count the number of customers
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers

nl: Show orders with total amount between 50 and 200
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE total_amount BETWEEN 50 AND 200

nl: List all returned orders
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'returned'

nl: Show products with a margin higher than $100
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE (list_price - cost_price) > 100

nl: List all order items that had a discount
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM order_items WHERE discount_pct > 0

nl: How many orders were completed or shipped?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status IN ('completed', 'shipped')

nl: List customers who signed up after June 1st 2023
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE signup_date > '2023-06-01'

nl: Show list of distinct payment methods used
expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT payment_method FROM orders

nl: Show the list price of iPhone 15
expected_sql: SELECT list_price FROM products WHERE name = 'iPhone 15'

nl: Show the customer with id 5
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE customer_id = 5

nl: Count the number of items sold with zero discount
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(quantity) FROM order_items WHERE discount_pct = 0

nl: What is the total spend of each customer?
expected_sql: SELECT c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.name

nl: Show the total revenue per product category
expected_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price), 2) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY revenue DESC

nl: Which customers live in the same city as Alice Smith?
expected_sql: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE city = (SELECT city FROM customers WHERE name = 'Alice Smith') AND name != 'Alice Smith'

nl: List the top 5 most expensive products
expected_sql: SELECT name, list_price FROM products ORDER BY list_price DESC LIMIT 5

nl: Count the number of orders by country
expected_sql: SELECT c.country, COUNT(o.order_id) FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.country

nl: What is the average order amount for customers in the US?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(o.total_amount) FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE c.country = 'US'

nl: Show monthly order counts for 2024
expected_sql: SELECT STRFTIME(order_date, '%Y-%m') AS month, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders WHERE YEAR(order_date) = 2024 GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

nl: Who are the top 10 customers by total spend?
expected_sql: SELECT c.name, c.email, ROUND(SUM(o.total_amount), 2) AS total_spend FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name, c.email ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 10

nl: What is the average order value by country?
expected_sql: SELECT c.country, ROUND(AVG(o.total_amount), 2) AS avg_order_value FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.country

nl: Show product categories and subcategories with their average price
expected_sql: SELECT category, subcategory, AVG(list_price) FROM products GROUP BY category, subcategory

nl: How many orders were placed by each customer?
expected_sql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY order_count DESC

nl: Find the total quantity sold for each product
expected_sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name

nl: List orders placed in the first quarter of 2024
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-03-31'

nl: What is the total quantity of items ordered in order id 1005?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(quantity) FROM order_items WHERE order_id = 1005

nl: Find products that have never been ordered
expected_sql: SELECT p.name FROM products p LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id WHERE oi.item_id IS NULL

nl: Show total orders by status for 2024
expected_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE YEAR(order_date) = 2024 GROUP BY status

nl: Calculate the total cost price and list price of all orders
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(p.cost_price * oi.quantity) AS total_cost, SUM(p.list_price * oi.quantity) AS total_list FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id

nl: Show the number of customers registered per country
expected_sql: SELECT country, COUNT(*) FROM customers GROUP BY country

nl: What is the most popular payment method in the US?
expected_sql: SELECT o.payment_method, COUNT(*) AS count FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE c.country = 'US' GROUP BY o.payment_method ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 1

nl: Show the total discounts given in each month of 2024
expected_sql: SELECT STRFTIME(o.order_date, '%Y-%m') AS month, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity * oi.discount_pct / 100.0) FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE YEAR(o.order_date) = 2024 GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

nl: Find all products in the category Electronics or Home
expected_sql: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category IN ('Electronics', 'Home')

nl: Show the top 3 cities with the highest total order spend
expected_sql: SELECT c.city, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spend FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.city ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 3

nl: List orders which total amount is above the average order amount
expected_sql: SELECT order_id, total_amount FROM orders WHERE total_amount > (SELECT AVG(total_amount) FROM orders)

nl: Find the customer who made the largest single order spend
expected_sql: SELECT c.name, o.total_amount FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id ORDER BY o.total_amount DESC LIMIT 1

nl: Count the number of items sold per category
expected_sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity) FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.category

nl: Show products that have a list price between $50 and $200 and cost price under $100
expected_sql: SELECT name FROM products WHERE list_price BETWEEN 50 AND 200 AND cost_price < 100

nl: List customers with an email containing gmail
expected_sql: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE email LIKE '%gmail.com%'

nl: What is the total quantity of order items for completed orders?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(oi.quantity) FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed'

nl: Show the average unit price of items sold in each category
expected_sql: SELECT p.category, AVG(oi.unit_price) FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.category

nl: List order ids where the shipping country is different from the customer country
expected_sql: SELECT o.order_id FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.shipping_country != c.country

nl: What is the return rate by category?
expected_sql: SELECT p.category, COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'returned' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS return_rate FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY return_rate DESC

nl: Show the top 3 subcategories by total sales volume
expected_sql: SELECT p.subcategory, SUM(oi.quantity) AS volume FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.subcategory ORDER BY volume DESC LIMIT 3

nl: Find the average cost price of products in each subcategory
expected_sql: SELECT subcategory, AVG(cost_price) FROM products GROUP BY subcategory

nl: List all customers who signed up in 2023 and had at least one order
expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE YEAR(c.signup_date) = 2023

nl: What is the total profit generated per category?
expected_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(SUM((oi.unit_price - p.cost_price) * oi.quantity), 2) AS profit FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY profit DESC

nl: What percentage of orders were cancelled or returned in each month of 2024?
expected_sql: SELECT STRFTIME(order_date, '%Y-%m') AS month, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN status IN ('cancelled','returned') THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS pct_cancelled_or_returned FROM orders WHERE YEAR(order_date) = 2024 GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

nl: Show the cumulative revenue generated in 2024
expected_sql: SELECT order_date, SUM(total_amount) OVER (ORDER BY order_date) AS cumulative_revenue FROM orders WHERE YEAR(order_date) = 2024

nl: Find customers who spent more than the average customer total spend
expected_sql: WITH customer_spends AS (SELECT customer_id, SUM(total_amount) AS total_spend FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT c.name, cs.total_spend FROM customer_spends cs JOIN customers c ON cs.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE cs.total_spend > (SELECT AVG(total_spend) FROM customer_spends)

nl: Rank products by revenue within each category
expected_sql: SELECT p.category, p.name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY p.category ORDER BY SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) DESC) AS rank FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.category, p.name

nl: Which customers have placed more than 5 orders?
expected_sql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name HAVING order_count > 5

nl: Calculate the average profit margin percentage per product category
expected_sql: SELECT category, ROUND(100.0 * AVG((list_price - cost_price) / list_price), 2) AS avg_margin_pct FROM products GROUP BY category

nl: Show customers who have purchased from all product categories
expected_sql: SELECT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT category) FROM products)

nl: Show year-over-year revenue comparison per category
expected_sql: WITH yearly_rev AS (SELECT p.category, YEAR(o.order_date) AS year, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id GROUP BY p.category, year) SELECT y1.category, y1.year, y1.revenue, y2.revenue AS prev_year_revenue, ROUND(100.0 * (y1.revenue - y2.revenue) / y2.revenue, 2) AS yoy_growth FROM yearly_rev y1 LEFT JOIN yearly_rev y2 ON y1.category = y2.category AND y1.year = y2.year + 1

nl: Identify products that constitute the top 20% of total revenue
expected_sql: WITH product_revenue AS (SELECT product_id, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items GROUP BY product_id), ranked_revenue AS (SELECT product_id, revenue, SUM(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY revenue DESC) AS running_rev, SUM(revenue) OVER () AS total_rev FROM product_revenue) SELECT p.name, rr.revenue FROM ranked_revenue rr JOIN products p ON rr.product_id = p.product_id WHERE rr.running_rev <= rr.total_rev * 0.20

nl: Find the repeat purchase rate (percentage of customers with >1 order)
expected_sql: WITH customer_orders AS (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN cnt > 1 THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS repeat_purchase_rate FROM customer_orders

nl: What is the average order item count for completed orders?
expected_sql: WITH order_counts AS (SELECT order_id, SUM(quantity) AS total_items FROM order_items GROUP BY order_id) SELECT AVG(oc.total_items) FROM order_counts oc JOIN orders o ON oc.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed'

nl: List products whose list price is at least 3 times their cost price
expected_sql: SELECT name, list_price, cost_price FROM products WHERE list_price >= 3 * cost_price

nl: Find orders containing items from more than 3 distinct product categories
expected_sql: SELECT oi.order_id, COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS cat_count FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY oi.order_id HAVING cat_count > 3

nl: Identify the top subcategory by revenue for each country
expected_sql: WITH subcat_rev AS (SELECT c.country, p.subcategory, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.country, p.subcategory), ranked_subcats AS (SELECT country, subcategory, revenue, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY revenue DESC) AS rn FROM subcat_rev) SELECT country, subcategory, revenue FROM ranked_subcats WHERE rn = 1

nl: What is the monthly growth rate of sales in 2024?
expected_sql: WITH monthly_sales AS (SELECT STRFTIME(order_date, '%Y-%m') AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE YEAR(order_date) = 2024 GROUP BY month) SELECT m1.month, m1.revenue, m2.revenue AS prev_month_revenue, ROUND(100.0 * (m1.revenue - m2.revenue) / m2.revenue, 2) AS growth_pct FROM monthly_sales m1 LEFT JOIN monthly_sales m2 ON m1.month = STRFTIME(CAST(m2.month || '-01' AS DATE) + INTERVAL 1 MONTH, '%Y-%m')

nl: Identify orders that were shipped more than 5 days after order date
expected_sql: SELECT order_id, customer_id FROM orders WHERE status = 'shipped' AND (order_date + INTERVAL 5 DAY) < order_date

nl: Find the average order value of customers who registered in 2023 vs 2024
expected_sql: SELECT YEAR(c.signup_date) AS cohort, AVG(o.total_amount) FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY cohort

nl: Which products represent more than 10% of order items in quantity?
expected_sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS qty FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name HAVING qty > (SELECT SUM(quantity) * 0.1 FROM order_items)

nl: Compare average customer spend between US and other countries
expected_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN c.country = 'US' THEN 'US' ELSE 'International' END AS region, AVG(o.total_amount) FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY region

nl: Find products with above average profit margins within category
expected_sql: WITH margins AS (SELECT product_id, name, category, list_price - cost_price AS profit FROM products), avg_margins AS (SELECT category, AVG(list_price - cost_price) AS avg_profit FROM products GROUP BY category) SELECT m.name, m.category, m.profit FROM margins m JOIN avg_margins am ON m.category = am.category WHERE m.profit > am.avg_profit

nl: Show the top 5 product items with highest discount percentages and completed orders
expected_sql: SELECT p.name, oi.discount_pct FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' ORDER BY oi.discount_pct DESC LIMIT 5

nl: List order items with a unit price difference from list price of product
expected_sql: SELECT oi.item_id, p.name FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id WHERE ABS(oi.unit_price - p.list_price) > 0.01

nl: Find the running count of customers registered in each country by date
expected_sql: SELECT country, signup_date, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY signup_date) AS running_cust_count FROM customers

nl: Show the monthly order amount and the difference compared to previous month
expected_sql: WITH monthly_revenue AS (SELECT STRFTIME(order_date, '%Y-%m') AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders GROUP BY month) SELECT month, revenue, LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_revenue, revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS diff FROM monthly_revenue

nl: Calculate percentage contribution of each product category to total sales
expected_sql: WITH cat_sales AS (SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.category) SELECT category, ROUND(100.0 * revenue / (SELECT SUM(revenue) FROM cat_sales), 2) AS pct_contribution FROM cat_sales ORDER BY pct_contribution DESC
